name: vercel-deploy-claimable
description: Deploy applications and websites to Vercel. Use this skill when the user requests deployment actions such as 'Deploy my app', 'Deploy this to production', 'Create a preview deployment', 'Deploy and give me the link', or 'Push this live'. No authentication required - returns preview URL and claimable deployment link.
capabilities: Package projects into deployment-ready tarballs excluding node_modules and .git
Auto-detect frameworks from package.json including Next.js, React, Vue, Svelte, and more
Create preview deployments with live URLs
Generate claim URLs for transferring deployments to Vercel accounts
Handle static HTML projects by renaming files for proper routing
Output deployment results in both human-readable and JSON formats
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static analysis findings are false positives from pattern-matching on markdown documentation text. The skill is a legitimate DevOps tool that executes bash scripts and makes network calls to Vercel API, which is expected behavior for a deployment skill. No malicious patterns detected.
valueStatement: Deploying applications to production hosting is complex and time-consuming. This skill packages your project and deploys it to Vercel with a single command, providing instant preview URLs.
limitations: Requires network access to Vercel API endpoints
May require users to configure network capabilities in Claude.ai settings
Does not authenticate with Vercel account directly
Framework detection relies on package.json presence and contents

useCases.targetUser: Frontend developers working on React, Vue, or Next.js projects
useCases.title: Quick Preview Deployments
useCases.description: Developers can instantly create preview deployments of their projects to share with team members or clients before merging to production.

useCases.targetUser: Designers and content creators with static websites
useCases.title: Static Site Hosting
useCases.description: Users can deploy static HTML websites to Vercel without needing to set up a Vercel account or configure deployment pipelines.

useCases.title: CI/CD Integration Testing
useCases.description: Teams can use this skill to test deployment configurations and verify builds before integrating with formal CI/CD pipelines.

promptTemplates.title: Deploy Current Project
promptTemplates.prompt: Deploy my current project to Vercel and give me the preview URL.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner - User wants to quickly deploy their working directory

promptTemplates.title: Deploy Specific Directory
promptTemplates.prompt: Deploy the project at /path/to/my-app to Vercel.
promptTemplates.scenario: Intermediate - User has multiple projects and wants to deploy a specific one

promptTemplates.title: Deploy and Share Results
promptTemplates.prompt: Deploy my Next.js app and format the output with both the preview URL and claim URL for my team.
promptTemplates.scenario: Intermediate - User needs deployment results in a shareable format

promptTemplates.title: Troubleshoot Deployment Failure
promptTemplates.prompt: My Vercel deployment failed with a network error. Help me configure my Claude.ai network settings and retry the deployment.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced - User encounters network restrictions and needs assistance resolving them

bestPractices: Review the preview URL before sharing with clients or deploying to production
Use the claim URL to transfer ownership of deployments to your Vercel account for long-term hosting
Ensure network capabilities are enabled in Claude.ai settings before deploying
antiPatterns: Do not deploy projects containing sensitive data like API keys or credentials
Do not rely on preview URLs for production traffic without claiming the deployment
Do not deploy large projects without checking Vercel's size limits first

faq.question: Do I need a Vercel account to use this skill?
faq.answer: No account is required for preview deployments. You receive a preview URL to view your site and a claim URL to transfer the deployment to your Vercel account if desired.

faq.question: What frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: The skill auto-detects popular frameworks including Next.js, React, Vue, Nuxt, Svelte, SvelteKit, Astro, Solid, Angular, Express, Fastify, NestJS, Vite, and many others from your package.json.

faq.question: Why is network access required?
faq.answer: The deployment script must communicate with Vercel's API to upload your project and create deployments. Enable *.vercel.com in your Claude.ai network settings.

faq.question: How do I claim my deployment?
faq.answer: Visit the claim URL provided in the deployment output. This transfers the deployment to your Vercel account where you can manage it like any other project.

faq.question: What files are included in the deployment?
faq.answer: All project files are included except node_modules and .git directories. The tarball excludes these to reduce upload size since Vercel handles dependency installation.

faq.question: Can I deploy static HTML sites without package.json?
faq.answer: Yes. For static HTML projects, the skill packages all files directly. If you have a single HTML file not named index.html, it will be renamed to ensure proper routing.
